content_markdown:
The current in the circuit shown below is constant when the switch is closed.

The energy transfer in the internal resistance $r$ of the battery is $15\text{ J}$ when a charge of $40\text{ C}$ passes through it. For the same amount of charge, $45\text{ J}$ of energy is transferred in the resistor $R$.

Which of the following gives the e.m.f. of the battery?

diagram_metadata.description: A circuit diagram containing a battery with internal resistance r, an external resistor R, and a switch, all connected in a single series loop.

vibe_explanation: The electromotive force (e.m.f.) is defined as the total energy supplied by the battery per unit charge. In this circuit, energy is dissipated in both the internal resistance $r$ and the external resistor $R$. The total energy transferred for a charge of $40\text{ C}$ is the sum of the energy in $r$ and $R$, which is $15\text{ J} + 45\text{ J} = 60\text{ J}$. Using the formula $\epsilon = \frac{W}{Q}$, we get $\epsilon = \frac{60}{40}\text{ V}$.
